title: Writing a review
explanation:
Writing a review

A good review describes something, evaluates it and gives a recommendation.

  • Include basic facts.
  • Mention positive and negative points.
  • Use adjectives and clear opinion language.
  • End with a recommendation.

questions.text: [*The film is set in the future and has an unusual storyline.|The film future unusual storyline.|Film in future unusual.]
questions.feedback: A review should describe the content clearly.

questions.text: [*Although the acting is strong, the plot feels slow in the middle.|Although acting strong plot slow middle.|Acting strong although plot.]
[*I would recommend it to anyone who enjoys science fiction.|I recommend anyone enjoys science fiction.|Recommend to anyone science fiction.]
[*Overall, it is entertaining but slightly too long.|Overall entertaining but too long little.|Overall too long entertaining.]

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: A review should include both positive and ____ comments. | *negative | casual | secret
Useful review adjectives include gripping, disappointing and ____. | *moving | receipt | arrival
At the end, say whether you would ____ it. | *recommend | interrupt | replace
A strong opening sentence often gives the basic ____ about the film or book. | *facts | complaints | customs
questions.feedback: Facts such as title or type.

questions.text: [*The soundtrack was excellent, which made several scenes more powerful.|Soundtrack excellent scenes more powerful.|The soundtrack more powerful excellent.]
[*One weakness is that some characters are underdeveloped.|One weakness some characters underdeveloped.|Weakness is characters underdeveloping.]
[*If you enjoy thoughtful dramas, this is worth watching.|If enjoy thoughtful dramas worth watching.|Enjoy dramas this worth.]
[*Despite a slow start, the series becomes more engaging later on.|Despite slow start series more engaging later.|Despite start slow later engaging.]
